title: Sanji and Zeff’s Starvation Flashback
description: On pages 13–16, Sanji’s past with Zeff is revealed through their starvation on a rock, explaining the debt that binds him to the Baratie.
scene: The flashback strips away the noise of battle and focuses on hunger, sacrifice, and the dream of creating a restaurant where no one is denied food.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: So you woke up, did you, you little eggplant...

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: You're a pretty lucky one, alright.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: P-Pira...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Dwawawawawawawawawawaww!

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Of course you can't move easily. Just whose kick did you think you received?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Where's the ship!?

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: who knows.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: wouldn't be surprising if your ship sank during that rough storm.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But... I know my ship has sunk for sure at least.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I can tell by the wreckage.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I can only be grateful that I didn't see any of my crewmates' dead bodies.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: In any case, the storm was 2 days ago.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Meaning that you and I,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Are stuck on this god-forsaken rock,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: without a single plant, fruit, or animal to eat.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Of course, there's a lot of fish in the ocean,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But the side of this rock's been carved out by the waves,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So that once you jump down, it'll be impossible to come back up,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: In other words, we don't have any way to get food. Feel free to cry if you want.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: No.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This is all your fault! I'll kill you for this!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If you don't shut up, I'll just eat you instead.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Waiting for help is the only option we have.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If we're lucky, we might be saved tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: And if we're not, we'll be waiting until we're nothing but a dried lip pile of bones.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Pile of Bones?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This is your share.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This food here got washed up here just like us.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Eating normally, it should last you 5 days,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: It's a good thing that we're both cooks.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But use your head and save as much as you can.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Wait a minute! Your share is way bigger than mine! It's almost 3 times what I've got!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Of course! I'm an adult, so it's only natural that I eat more.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That's the situation we're stuck in.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So let's just try to get along until help comes, alright?

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: DESCRIPTION:

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: You damn geezer...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You should consider me nice for even giving you food in the first place.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So don't expect me to be any nicer than that.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You sit on this side,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: And I'll sit on that other side of the rock. This way, we'll be able to keep watch on both sides of the sea.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If you see a ship, call and let me know right away.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Until then, don't bother contacting me.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Best not to waste any energy for unnecessary things.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Pray for good luck.

summary: This part gives emotional meaning to Sanji’s choices: Zeff’s sacrifice explains why feeding the hungry is sacred to him.
